items.summary: Fermi Inc. has issued $431.25 million in aggregate principal amount of 5.00% convertible senior notes due 2031, with total net proceeds of approximately $416.81 million, which will be used for general corporate purposes. The notes are convertible into cash, shares of common stock, or a combination thereof, at the company's election, with an initial conversion price of approximately $9.52 per share. The company also entered into capped call transactions to reduce potential dilution to its common stock upon conversion of the notes, using approximately $34.5 million of the net proceeds to pay for these transactions.

items.summary: Fermi Inc. discloses the immediate resignation of director Miles Everson, effective July 10, 2026, due to a disagreement over access to company books and records and the delegation of financing transaction oversight to the Finance Committee. Everson's resignation was submitted in a letter, which is attached to the filing as an exhibit, and he did not serve on any committee of the Board at the time of his resignation. The company believes Everson was granted full access to its books and records, except for certain litigation-related materials, and had previously approved the establishment of the Finance Committee.

items.summary: Fermi Inc. commenced an offering for the sale of $350 million aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes due 2031 to qualified institutional buyers, with the option to purchase an additional $52.5 million. The company intends to use the net proceeds to pay for capped call transactions and for general corporate purposes. Fermi Inc. also disclosed that it has deferred its election to be taxed as a real estate investment trust, and now expects to make this election for its taxable year ending December 31, 2026.

Neugebauer, Vicksburg Investments Management LLC, and other parties have withdrawn their solicitation to call a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders due to an untenable timeline caused by a judge's recusal in pending litigation. Neugebauer, co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., has suspended his current proxy campaign to call a special meeting of shareholders due to a judge's last-minute recusal, which has disrupted the timeline for the strategic process. Neugebauer's campaign had earned the backing of over 70% of votes cast and two leading proxy advisory firms, and he remains confident in Fermi's ability to secure a tenant group and achieve long-term success. Neugebauer will continue to push for good governance through the judicial process and monitor the board's execution on Project Matador to ensure maximum value for stakeholders.

Neugebauer emphasized the importance of having the right team to steward these contracts and ensure the company's success, and he advocated for a dual-track process and evidence-based decision-making in evaluating potential tenants.
